$comment: Machine-readable spec of the loop-vibe-coding state-machine table. An AI agent (via any Lark Base API or MCP) can consume this to create the table deterministically. Field 'name' values are the DEFAULT column names; they map to the logical keys in loop.config.yaml > base.fields. After creation, write the resulting app_token and table_id back into loop.config.yaml.

table.name: loop-vibe-coding
table.description: One row per change. The `owner` field is the relay baton between coder and reviewer; the `status` field is the lifecycle stage.

fields.name: change_id
fields.desc: OpenSpec change identifier, filled after the spec is written.

fields.name: status
fields.desc: Lifecycle stage. Agents only write 'done' from the 'integrating' stage (after a human accepts); the human writes status on kickoff and at the acceptance gate. 'blocked' is the circuit-breaker parking lane — an agent moves a non-converging change here for a human decision (it is NOT the acceptance lane).

fields.name: owner
fields.desc: The relay baton. The human sets it on kickoff and at the acceptance gate; agents pass it on hand-off. Cleared (left empty) when a change reaches 'done'.

fields.name: spec_ref
fields.desc: Spec content or a link to it.

fields.name: review
fields.desc: Structured, severity-tagged feedback. Appended per round.

fields.name: resolution
fields.desc: Per-item judgement of feedback + what was changed.

fields.name: round
fields.desc: Review iteration counter (spec + code reviews). Drives the max_rounds circuit breaker. Each writer has one specific job: the reviewer increments it on every review and resets it to 0 on spec approval (spec->implementing); a human resets it to 0 on a bug bounce (testing->implementing); the coder NEVER increments — it only force-resets to 0 to self-heal a human bug bounce that left a stale non-zero value. No role writes round outside these cases.

fields.name: test_report
fields.desc: Raw machine test/lint output (written by the coder each self-check) + the human's acceptance notes.

fields.name: bug
fields.desc: Repro steps logged when bouncing a change back from testing.

fields.name: claimed_by
fields.desc: Optimistic lock. Before working a row, an agent writes a unique run token here (e.g. 'reviewer:run-<uuid>'), then re-reads: it only proceeds if the token is still its own. Two overlapping ticks write different tokens; the last write wins on re-read, so only one agent claims the row. Cleared on hand-off.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.desc: Auto timestamp; pollers pick the oldest pending record.

views.name: Needs me
views.desc: Your inbox: rows awaiting acceptance test or a tie-break.

post_create: Write the created base app_token into loop.config.yaml > base.app_token.
Write the created table_id into loop.config.yaml > base.table_id.
If any column name differs from the defaults above, update loop.config.yaml > base.fields accordingly.
Optional: in Lark Base, add an automation that sends a message when `owner` becomes `human`.
